scenarios.name: Coffee Shop
scenarios.description: Order coffee and cafe drinks easily
scenarios.tip: Portugal coffee stops often happen at a cafe or pastelaria counter, so simple greeting-first ordering, water, takeaway, and bill language matter more than heavy drink customization.

scenarios.name: Street Food / Restaurant
scenarios.tip: For Portugal, treat this as cafe, snack-bar, takeaway, and casual restaurant language; the practical asks are menu clarity, water, the bill, and simple food adjustments rather than bargaining.

scenarios.name: Ride App / Taxi
scenarios.tip: Showing the destination on your phone still helps, especially for station pickups, airport transfers, hotel drop-offs, and short city rides when the driver replies quickly.

scenarios.name: Asking the Price
scenarios.tip: Use this mostly for price confirmation and item choice; marked prices usually matter more than negotiation language in Portugal.

scenarios.name: Polite Basics
scenarios.tip: Greeting first is part of the utility in Portugal, so hello, thank you, excuse me, and no thank you deserve first-wave priority.

scenarios.name: Convenience Store
scenarios.tip: Keep this scenario practical: water, bag, receipt, card payment, and simple item-finding help do more work than browsing-heavy shopping vocabulary.

scenarios.name: Hotel / Hostel
scenarios.tip: Keep your passport and booking details ready and prioritize reservation, check-in, checkout, luggage help, and simple room-problem language first.

scenarios.name: Directions
scenarios.tip: Save your hotel, station, or airport stop on your phone because Portugal-friendly direction help often depends on station, metro, stop, or nearby-landmark language.

scenarios.name: Simple Problems
scenarios.tip: If something goes wrong, start with repair phrases and show booking screens, tickets, or screenshots before trying longer explanations.

scenarios.name: Small Talk
scenarios.tip: Keep small talk light and secondary; practical travel utility should be authored before conversation filler.

notes: Shared traveler baseline scaffold for Portugal-focused European Portuguese, kept prep-only and reprioritized toward greetings, repair, transport, hotels, payment, bathroom help, and bill requests before runtime wiring.
